Almost came upon this museum by accident and decided to take a tour.  It is a very unusual concept as you 'select' a family whose background you wish to hear about and get taken on a tour of the tenement apartment they lived in, see artefacts and items used by the family and hear testimonies about the hardships and daily struggle and how it was living in those times.  Our tour leader Caitlin was very knowledgeable and made it so interesting and the whole experience was well worth doing although slightly on the pricey side.

Wonderful! I'd been strongly recommended to go, but was even better, The 2A Moores Irish family.  The depth and detail of the information, the immersive physical experience in the rooms (not tactile! you're not allowed to touch! - or snack, don't encourage return of the rats) and the enthusiasm and  sensitivity of our guide,  & she was very happy to answer questions & respond to the memories of visitors.  The bookshop is impressive, so many histories but also novels & memories of migrants. Well worth the cost, small groups with a dedicated guide.  I shall go again to see other families. What a great formula for a 'museum'.

This is a very different type of museum.
Our tour was centered around a street and a flat in a restored house in Lower East Side, Manhattan which in 1902 was home to the Levines a Russian immigrant family. 
Our educator, Grace was very knowledgeable and enthusiastically guided us through their life and that of the neighborhood, it was very interesting and enjoyable.
There are a number of other tours to choose from covering different times and different cultures. Well worth the time.
